# See the Early Stages of How ‘Project Hail Mary’ Brought Rocky to Life

Concept artists Kris Anka and Jesús Alonso Iglesias provided a look into the development of everyone's favorite new alien.


By
[Justin Carter](https://gizmodo.com/author/gigawattconduit)
Published April 5, 2026, 10:30 am ET


Reading time 2 minutes


![Project Hail Mary Rocky](https://gizmodo.com/app/uploads/2026/03/project-hail-mary-rocky.jpg)

© Amazon MGM


Read LaterRead Later

[Comments\\
(15)](https://gizmodo.com/see-the-early-stages-of-how-project-hail-mary-brought-rocky-to-life-2000742281#comments)

Whether you knew it from the trailers or having read the book, _[Project Hail Mary](https://gizmodo.com/project-hail-mary-directors-commentary-theaterears-2000742154)_ also stars an alien rock named [Rocky](https://gizmodo.com/project-hail-mary-rocky-merchandise-where-to-buy-2000740615). Directors Phil Lord and Chris Miller have previously spoken about making him using practical puppets, and now concept art shows what else he could’ve looked like in the film.

On BlueSky, artist [Kris Anka](https://www.instagram.com/p/DWrmrJjkidP/), who’s previously done work for the _Spider-Verse_ movies (and drawn hot [DC and Marvel characters](https://www.instagram.com/kristaferanka/p/DWZuDD_krpC/)) showed off some early Rocky designs he made in 2023. According to him, the art team had to “find interesting ways to play with asymmetry, silhouette, and texture.” While following that directive, Anka also looked at different ways to establish “depict culture,” hence having design 2C having barnacles for decoration and to denote his rank.

[kristaferanka](https://www.instagram.com/kristaferanka/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=11152e25-60c2-4f4e-8dae-531c273e080c)

A few years ago I was asked to be a small part of [#projecthailmary](https://www.instagram.com/explore/tags/projecthailmary/?utm_source=ig_embed)

This is a selection of designs from my first few passes on Rocky, our main directive was to find interesting ways to play with asymmetry, silhouette and texture while I took some attempts to imbue ways to depict culture (gems and barnacles as decorative or identifying elements)

[View all 88 comments](https://www.instagram.com/p/DWrmrJjkidP/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=11152e25-60c2-4f4e-8dae-531c273e080c)

[Add a comment...](https://www.instagram.com/p/DWrmrJjkidP/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=11152e25-60c2-4f4e-8dae-531c273e080c) [_Instagram_](https://www.instagram.com/p/DWrmrJjkidP/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=11152e25-60c2-4f4e-8dae-531c273e080c)

In each design, Anka leans into the idea of Rocky having an arachnid-like body, particularly in designs 1E, 1G, and 1K. While most designs fit close to author Andy Weir’s description of Rocky as “smaller than a human, about the size of a Labrador,” it’s 2A, 1E, 1F, and 1I that keep his skin, said to be “weird, brownish-black rock.” The second round of designs feature notes that try to explain or make sense of his physiology: 2E gives him a crystal-shaped body and more defined mouth, and that and 2D give him patterned growths over what’s otherwise a conventional alien body.

After the Rocky puppet was made, Anka returned to help figure out [Rocky’s emotions](https://www.instagram.com/kristaferanka/p/DWuWslKEoPG/). There, he explored “different ways to use body acting, making sounds, \[and\] moving rocks around to evoke feelings.” Some of his work made it to screen, and he also helped figure out how Rocky’s fingers work.

[kristaferanka](https://www.instagram.com/kristaferanka/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=58520ded-117d-411c-a9ff-46fa49776002)

so about a year later I was asked back to help on [#projecthailmary](https://www.instagram.com/explore/tags/projecthailmary/?utm_source=ig_embed) , this time they had built a rough puppet already but still had a few things they wanted help to figure out. Where I think I really had a helping hand on was how Rocky would emote: different ways to use body acting, angles and leans, ways to make sound, or even moving rocks around to evoke feelings. There’s definitely a few of these acting drawings I think I saw on screen. Another question they had was how Rockys fingers work, so had worked up a few pitches for that as well.

[View all 10 comments](https://www.instagram.com/p/DWuWslKEoPG/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=58520ded-117d-411c-a9ff-46fa49776002)

[Add a comment...](https://www.instagram.com/p/DWuWslKEoPG/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=58520ded-117d-411c-a9ff-46fa49776002) [_Instagram_](https://www.instagram.com/p/DWuWslKEoPG/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=58520ded-117d-411c-a9ff-46fa49776002)

Anka wasn’t the only artist to show off Rocky designs. Artist [Jesús Alonso Iglesias](https://www.instagram.com/jesusalonsoiglesias/p/DWoMEQcitfl/) went for a design with a rounder “head” full of holes and long legs not unlike Anka’s 2E and 2D. Iglesias’ art shows us his Rocky would’ve used his hands similar to humans (to the point of doing a Rubik’s Cube) and sleep in ways that made him look alien. Designing Rocky was “one of the most difficult \[challenges\] I’ve worked on so far,” but one that taught him about character design.

![](https://scontent-lax3-1.cdninstagram.com/v/t51.82787-15/659634369_18429553567142158_1142776254564405751_n.jpg?stp=c343.0.753.753a_dst-jpg_e35_s240x240_tt6&efg=eyJ2ZW5jb2RlX3RhZyI6ImltYWdlX3VybGdlbi4xNDQweDc1My5zZHIuZjgyNzg3LmRlZmF1bHRfaW1hZ2UuYzIifQ&_nc_ht=scontent-lax3-1.cdninstagram.com&_nc_cat=110&_nc_oc=Q6cZ2gEX8piwrDabWGdElWI0fIov6ZsFqkVoCXpsfvKa0pUTkZbzUG4Ripx7JWKhuXyWk5A&_nc_ohc=a6t9rzTOtpkQ7kNvwG7vv3M&_nc_gid=RMMt1WNQcmk28vFzEjkHVg&edm=APs17CUBAAAA&ccb=7-5&oh=00_Af3SLPmX37JjCVPMht1V5x0Mwn4qtunbOXsp0n0uhIElqQ&oe=69DA9E71&_nc_sid=10d13b)![](https://scontent-lax3-1.cdninstagram.com/v/t51.82787-15/656232361_18427982866142158_7766514380948888772_n.jpg?stp=c0.240.1440.1440a_dst-jpegr_e35_s240x240_tt6&efg=eyJ2ZW5jb2RlX3RhZyI6ImltYWdlX3VybGdlbi4xNDQweDE5MjAuaGRyLmY4Mjc4Ny5kZWZhdWx0X2ltYWdlLmMyIn0&_nc_ht=scontent-lax3-1.cdninstagram.com&_nc_cat=110&_nc_oc=Q6cZ2gEX8piwrDabWGdElWI0fIov6ZsFqkVoCXpsfvKa0pUTkZbzUG4Ripx7JWKhuXyWk5A&_nc_ohc=2FksT1zegfEQ7kNvwG4FSeV&_nc_gid=RMMt1WNQcmk28vFzEjkHVg&edm=APs17CUBAAAA&ccb=7-5&oh=00_Af33JIGHnoRYjkvyU4xcOIVjB5pglHuDLkY3BNbN9O3BuQ&oe=69DAB51E&_nc_sid=10d13b)](https://www.instagram.com/jesusalonsoiglesias/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=0b864540-e978-4495-b877-f60633e0da40)

[6,833 likes](https://www.instagram.com/p/DWoMEQcitfl/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=0b864540-e978-4495-b877-f60633e0da40)

[jesusalonsoiglesias](https://www.instagram.com/jesusalonsoiglesias/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=0b864540-e978-4495-b877-f60633e0da40)

Some time ago I was lucky enough to be part of the incredible team that designed the character of Rocky in the film [#ProjectHailMary](https://www.instagram.com/explore/tags/ProjectHailMary/?utm_source=ig_embed). It was just a few weeks but the experience was a blast. The challenge of visualizing such a complicated character with so many nuances has been one of the most difficult ones I’ve worked on so far. The ideas put forward by all the artists on the team were wonderful and inspiring. And during that short experience I learned a lot about how to deal with character design. Abstract thinking, getting out of the obvious, thinking different. Thank you so much to [@phillordy](https://www.instagram.com/phillordy/?utm_source=ig_embed) and [@chrizmillr](https://www.instagram.com/chrizmillr/?utm_source=ig_embed) for letting me be a small part of this wonderful experience and this f\*\*king great movie. Go and watch Project Hail Mary!

[View all 47 comments](https://www.instagram.com/p/DWoMEQcitfl/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=0b864540-e978-4495-b877-f60633e0da40)

[Add a comment...](https://www.instagram.com/p/DWoMEQcitfl/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=0b864540-e978-4495-b877-f60633e0da40) [_Instagram_](https://www.instagram.com/p/DWoMEQcitfl/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=0b864540-e978-4495-b877-f60633e0da40)

The Rocky designs Anka and Iglesias released make us think about what _Project Hail Mary_ would’ve looked like if the team went in a different direction for its co-lead…and we’d also like for the film’s art book to drop so we can see what else the film could’ve looked like. Hopefully such a book is coming in time for the movie’s physical release, or even the holiday season.

Skip to content
Movies

See the Early Stages of How ‘Project Hail Mary’ Brought Rocky to Life

Concept artists Kris Anka and Jesús Alonso Iglesias provided a look into the development of everyone's favorite new alien.
By Justin Carter Published April 5, 2026, 10:30 am ET

Reading time 2 minutes

Comments (15)

Whether you knew it from the trailers or having read the book, Project Hail Mary also stars an alien rock named Rocky. Directors Phil Lord and Chris Miller have previously spoken about making him using practical puppets, and now concept art shows what else he could’ve looked like in the film.

On BlueSky, artist Kris Anka, who’s previously done work for the Spider-Verse movies (and drawn hot DC and Marvel characters) showed off some early Rocky designs he made in 2023. According to him, the art team had to “find interesting ways to play with asymmetry, silhouette, and texture.” While following that directive, Anka also looked at different ways to establish “depict culture,” hence having design 2C having barnacles for decoration and to denote his rank.

Instagram
kristaferanka
141K followers
View profile
    kristaferanka
    2,911 posts · 141K followers
    View more on Instagram
    LikeCommentShareSave
    24,411 likes
    kristaferanka

    A few years ago I was asked to be a small part of #projecthailmary
    This is a selection of designs from my first few passes on Rocky, our main directive was to find interesting ways to play with asymmetry, silhouette and texture while I took some attempts to imbue ways to depict culture (gems and barnacles as decorative or identifying elements)
    View all 88 comments
    Add a comment...Instagram

    In each design, Anka leans into the idea of Rocky having an arachnid-like body, particularly in designs 1E, 1G, and 1K. While most designs fit close to author Andy Weir’s description of Rocky as “smaller than a human, about the size of a Labrador,” it’s 2A, 1E, 1F, and 1I that keep his skin, said to be “weird, brownish-black rock.” The second round of designs feature notes that try to explain or make sense of his physiology: 2E gives him a crystal-shaped body and more defined mouth, and that and 2D give him patterned growths over what’s otherwise a conventional alien body.

    After the Rocky puppet was made, Anka returned to help figure out Rocky’s emotions. There, he explored “different ways to use body acting, making sounds, [and] moving rocks around to evoke feelings.” Some of his work made it to screen, and he also helped figure out how Rocky’s fingers work.

    Instagram
    kristaferanka
    141K followers
    View profile
      kristaferanka
      2,911 posts · 141K followers
      View more on Instagram
      LikeCommentShareSave
      4,567 likes
      kristaferanka

      so about a year later I was asked back to help on #projecthailmary , this time they had built a rough puppet already but still had a few things they wanted help to figure out. Where I think I really had a helping hand on was how Rocky would emote: different ways to use body acting, angles and leans, ways to make sound, or even moving rocks around to evoke feelings. There’s definitely a few of these acting drawings I think I saw on screen. Another question they had was how Rockys fingers work, so had worked up a few pitches for that as well.
      View all 10 comments
      Add a comment...Instagram

      Anka wasn’t the only artist to show off Rocky designs. Artist Jesús Alonso Iglesias went for a design with a rounder “head” full of holes and long legs not unlike Anka’s 2E and 2D. Iglesias’ art shows us his Rocky would’ve used his hands similar to humans (to the point of doing a Rubik’s Cube) and sleep in ways that made him look alien. Designing Rocky was “one of the most difficult [challenges] I’ve worked on so far,” but one that taught him about character design.

      Instagram
      jesusalonsoiglesias
      47.8K followers
      View profile
        jesusalonsoiglesias
        339 posts · 48K followers
        View more on Instagram
        LikeCommentShareSave
        6,833 likes
        jesusalonsoiglesias

        Some time ago I was lucky enough to be part of the incredible team that designed the character of Rocky in the film #ProjectHailMary. It was just a few weeks but the experience was a blast. The challenge of visualizing such a complicated character with so many nuances has been one of the most difficult ones I’ve worked on so far. The ideas put forward by all the artists on the team were wonderful and inspiring. And during that short experience I learned a lot about how to deal with character design. Abstract thinking, getting out of the obvious, thinking different. Thank you so much to @phillordy and @chrizmillr for letting me be a small part of this wonderful experience and this f**king great movie. Go and watch Project Hail Mary!
        View all 47 comments
        Add a comment...Instagram

        The Rocky designs Anka and Iglesias released make us think about what Project Hail Mary would’ve looked like if the team went in a different direction for its co-lead…and we’d also like for the film’s art book to drop so we can see what else the film could’ve looked like. Hopefully such a book is coming in time for the movie’s physical release, or even the holiday season.
